title: Fix scheduler: suppress all legacy followups when planner-owned set is complete
description: The scheduler followup suppression logic is too narrow: it only suppresses legacy auto-followups when ALL planner-owned shared followups exist. When only a subset exists (e.g. planner created a shared tester but not docs/review), it creates legacy children for the missing types, resulting in duplicate work. Fix in src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py: when checking whether to suppress auto-followup creation for a completing developer bead, suppress each followup type individually — if a planner-owned shared tester bead depending on this developer bead exists, suppress the -test auto-followup; if a planner-owned shared review bead exists, suppress the -review auto-followup, etc. The current test at tests/test_orchestrator.py line 353 (test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children) should pass after this fix.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler suppresses -test auto-followup when a planner-owned shared tester bead exists for that developer bead
Scheduler suppresses -review/-docs auto-followups independently based on planner-owned coverage
When no planner-owned followups exist, all auto-followups are created as before

handoff_summary.completed: Modified `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` lines 830–893: added `and not uses_planner_owned` guard to all three legacy followup creation branches (tester, documentation, review), and changed `else:` to `elif X is not None:` so scope-sync still fires for present planner-owned beads.
handoff_summary.risks: Planner-owned flows will no longer create docs/review legacy beads when those types are absent from the planner's design. This is intentional — the planner owns the followup graph. Standalone (non-planner-owned) developer flows are unaffected.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tests to confirm test_scheduler_does_not_backfill_legacy_children_when_shared_followups_exist and all related tests pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Only `scheduler.py` modified; no other files touched.

conflict_risks: Only `scheduler.py` modified; no other files touched.

execution_history.summary: Fixed scheduler followup suppression in `_create_followups()`: when `uses_planner_owned=True`, no legacy auto-followup beads are created for any type (test/docs/review), even if only a subset of planner-owned followup types exist. Changed `if test_bead is None:` → `if test_bead is None and not uses_planner_owned:` (and `else:` → `elif test_bead is not None:`) for all three followup types. This ensures the `else` branch (`_sync_followup_scope`) still fires for planner-owned beads that do exist.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
